perturbation.original_text: 1.13 "Program Errors" means any defect in the Flix Software that 1) results in the loss or corruption of data on a user's system, 2) causes the Flix Software to become unstable, 3) eliminates the user's ability to perform intended functions in the Flix Software, or 4) causes the operating system or any other programs to become unstable or cease to function. The defect must be caused by the Flix Software in the form provided by Wildform as a Deliverable.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 "Program Errors" means any defect in the Flix Software.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific criteria that define 'Program Errors,' the contract creates ambiguity about what constitutes a Program Error. This omission contradicts Section 3.2, where Wildform is obligated to resolve Program Errors, because without clearly defined criteria, determining whether Wildform has failed to meet its obligations becomes subjective and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Program Errors. During the Term, Wildform shall resolve Program Errors in the manner provided in Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific definition of "Program Errors", including criteria like data loss, instability, and impaired functionality. The changed text eliminates these criteria, broadly defining "Program Errors" as any defect. This creates a contradiction with Section 3.2, which obligates Wildform to resolve Program Errors. Without the specific criteria, it becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable to determine whether Wildform has failed to resolve a Program Error as per Exhibit A, because any defect could be considered a Program Error now, making the resolution process unclear and possibly triggering disagreements and legal disputes, thus causing in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Level of Transition Services. Wildform shall perform the Transition Services for which it is responsible hereunder following commonly accepted standards of care in the industry and exercising the same degree of care as it exercises in performing the same or similar services for its own business as of the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Level of Transition Services. Wildform shall perform the Transition Services for which it is responsible hereunder, as it deems appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the Level of Transition Services, removing the obligation for Wildform to follow 'commonly accepted standards of care in the industry', section 2.4 now contradicts Article VIII Indemnification, where Wildform's Indemnification of On2 is based on any breach by Wildform of Sections 5, 6 or 7 of this Agreement, because with the change in section 2.4, Wildform doesn't have to be follow the accepted standards and Indemnification could be difficult to be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Wildform's Indemnification of On2. Wildform agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless On2 from and against any claim, action, damage or costs (including reasonable attorneys fees) (other than an action or claim which is subject to On2's indemnification of Wildform pursuant to Section 8.1 above) based on any breach by Wildform of Sections 5, 6 or 7 of this Agreement, provided that: (i) Wildform is promptly notified in writing of such claim or action, (ii) On2 grants Wildform sole control of the defense and any related settlement negotiations, and (iii) On2 reasonably cooperates with Wildform in defense of such claim.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 2.4 required Wildform to perform Transition Services following 'commonly accepted standards of care in the industry.' The changed text removes this obligation, allowing Wildform to perform the services 'as it deems appropriate.' This creates a direct contradiction with section 8.2, which bases Wildform's indemnification of On2 on any breach by Wildform of Sections 5, 6 or 7 of this Agreement. If Wildform doesn't have to follow industry standards as defined in 2.4, it makes it difficult to enforce section 8.2 related to the standards and could severely limit its scope.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Deliverables. Subject to payment by On2 of the Closing Date Payment Amount in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement, Wildform shall deliver each Deliverable in accordance with the timeframe (the "Timeframe") set forth in Exhibit B, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement. On2 will use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with Wildform as necessary in completing the Development Work and delivery of the Deliverables. Upon receipt of each Deliverable, On2 shall have a period of thirty (30) working days within which to inspect such Deliverable based upon the relevant Specification (an "Acceptance Period"). Should On2 find that any Deliverable does not substantially conform to the relevant Specification, On2 shall promptly so notify Wildform in writing, which notice shall provide sufficiently detailed explanation of the non-conformities so as to allow Wildform to reproduce them (any such notice is referred to herein as a "Nonconformance Notice"). Each of the Deliverables shall be deemed accepted by On2 unless On2 delivers to Wildform a Nonconformance Notice within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of such Deliverable. In the event that Wildform receives such Nonconformance Notice from On2, Wildform shall make commercially reasonable efforts to modify such Deliverable and re-deliver it to On2 after any such modification is completed. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to have been accepted by On2 when either (i) On2 notifies Wildform in writing of its acceptance of such Deliverables or (ii) On2 does not deliver a Nonconformance Notice to Wildform within the Acceptance Period. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, On2 shall not unreasonably withhold acceptance of any Deliverable.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Deliverables. Subject to payment by On2 of the Closing Date Payment Amount in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement, Wildform shall deliver each Deliverable in accordance with the timeframe (the "Timeframe") set forth in Exhibit B, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement. On2 will use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with Wildform as necessary in completing the Development Work and delivery of the Deliverables. Upon receipt of each Deliverable, On2 shall have a period to inspect such Deliverable. Each of the Deliverables shall be deemed accepted by On2. In the event that Wildform receives such Nonconformance Notice from On2, Wildform shall make commercially reasonable efforts to modify such Deliverable and re-deliver it to On2 after any such modification is completed. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to have been accepted by On2 when either (i) On2 notifies Wildform in writing of its acceptance of such Deliverables or (ii) On2 does not deliver a Nonconformance Notice to Wildform within the Acceptance Period.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the 'relevant Specification' to be use on the Deliverables inspection, creates ambiguity and unenforceable because now there is no specific instruction to follow. This omission creates ambiguity to evaluate Article VIII Indemnification, where Wildform's Indemnification of On2 is based on any breach by Wildform of Sections 5, 6 or 7 of this Agreement. Also the timeframe of thirty (30) working days within which to inspect such Deliverable (an "Acceptance Period") was removed as well, therefore, the inspection period now isn't clear when it should take place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Wildform's Indemnification of On2. Wildform agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless On2 from and against any claim, action, damage or costs (including reasonable attorneys fees) (other than an action or claim which is subject to On2's indemnification of Wildform pursuant to Section 8.1 above) based on any breach by Wildform of Sections 5, 6 or 7 of this Agreement, provided that: (i) Wildform is promptly notified in writing of such claim or action, (ii) On2 grants Wildform sole control of the defense and any related settlement negotiations, and (iii) On2 reasonably cooperates with Wildform in defense of such claim.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'relevant Specification' for Deliverables inspection and the 'Acceptance Period' timeframe, contradicting section 8.2. This omission creates ambiguity and makes it difficult to determine if Wildform breached Sections 5, 6, or 7, as the standards for deliverable acceptance are now unclear, impacting On2's indemnification rights. The lack of a specific inspection timeframe further complicates enforcement and creates a direct conflict with the indemnification clause.
